name: Scallops & Shaved Fennel with Saffron, Honey & Citrus Vinaigrette
intro: Scallops are my favourite shellfish – delicate, sweet morsels of flesh that are so versatile you can eat them raw, seared, poached or baked. This is such an elegant dish, with a little crunch of shaved fennel and a sweet and aromatic dressing, pungent with saffron and citrus notes. Choose the fattest scallops you can find.

ingredients.item: large fennel bulbs, topped and tailed
sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

method: First make the dressing. Dissolve the honey and crumbled saffron threads in the vinegar and orange and lime juices in a small saucepan over a low-medium heat. Warm the mixture gently, allowing the saffron to infuse the liquid, but do not let it bubble or boil. Once the mixture begins to take on a golden colour from the saffron, give it a stir and season with a little sea salt. Turn off the heat and set aside.

Shave or thinly slice the fennel and place in a mixing bowl. Mix the lemon juice and 2 teaspoons of olive oil together, season with sea salt and black pepper, then toss over the fennel to prevent it from turning brown and set aside.

Preheat a large frying pan over a medium-high heat. Drizzle a little olive oil into the pan and pan-fry the scallops on each side for about 1 minute (or less, if you are using smaller scallops). Remove the scallops from the heat and arrange them on a serving platter with the shaved fennel.
Add the olive oil to the dressing and whisk well until it is amalgamated, then drizzle the dressing over the scallops and fennel and serve immediately.
